summary: Single-chain mapping for the Socket Gateway benchmark. Although Socket is a bridge aggregator (Bungee), the 2024-01-16 incident broke the in-chain swap path, so source_chain == destination_chain. Cross-chain mapping fields are kept for schema parity but collapse to ethereum.

destination_chain.name: ethereum
destination_chain.note: Same contract — Socket Gateway dispatches both same-chain swaps and cross-chain bridges through the same proxy.

entity_map.role: Aggregator proxy that dispatches to per-route swap implementations and holds user approvals.
Route #16 implementation. The bug-specific entry point. Modelled as SocketGateway.performAction in the benchmark contract.
Any user who had an outstanding approve(SocketGateway, ...) on a token at the time of the attack. ~219 victims observed in production.
Attacker EOA that drove the drain calls.

asset_map.notes: Largest single asset class drained. Same-chain (no bridge crossing).

invariants_for_fuzzer.description: performAction must require msg.sender == fromUser (or accept a signed permit binding the operation to a specific caller).

Tokens may only be pulled via transferFrom when the spender is acting on behalf of the original approver — i.e. ERC20.allowance[from][gateway] alone is not sufficient authorization without msg.sender == from.
For any user u who has not signed a transaction in block B, balanceOf(u) at end of block B >= balanceOf(u) at start of block B (modulo gas — no token movements).
